zh.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Martin VI, Baron of Athelhampton是Lady Agnes de Loundres的兒子，於1410年出生在英國多瑟特阿瑟爾哈普頓。他於1429年與Elizabeth Pydel在英國英格蘭多瑟特結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1470年在英國多瑟特阿瑟爾哈普頓享年60歲，埋葬在英國多瑟特阿瑟爾哈普頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Martin VI, Baron of Athelhampton是Lady Agnes de Loundres的儿子，于1410年出生在英国多瑟特阿瑟爾哈普頓。他于1429年与Elizabeth Pydel在英国英格兰多瑟特结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1470年在英国多瑟特阿瑟爾哈普頓享年60岁，埋葬在英国多瑟特阿瑟爾哈普頓。

nameMeanings.name: Martin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, Irish, French, Walloon, Breton, Dutch, Flemish, German, Czech, Slovak, Croatian, Italian (Veneto); Spanish (Martín): from a personal name derived from Latin Martinus, itself a derivative of Mars, genitive Martis, the Roman god of fertility and war, whose name may derive ultimately from a root mar ‘gleam’. This was borne by a famous 4th-century Christian saint, Martin of Tours, and consequently became extremely popular throughout Europe in the Middle Ages. In North America, the surname Martin has absorbed cognates and derivatives from other languages, e.g. Slovak and Rusyn (from Slovakia) Marcin , Albanian Martini , Polish surnames beginning with Marcin-, and Slovenian patronymics like Martinčič (see Martincic ). Martin is the most frequent surname in France and one of the most frequent surnames in Wallonia.
English: variant of   Marton  .
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Mártain, ‘descendant of Martin’ (compare 1 above). Otherwise, a shortened form of Gilmartin or McMartin ; sometimes also spelled Martyn.

variants.name: Marczewski
meanings: Polish: habitational name for someone from Marcewo (formerly also Marczewo) in Greater Poland Voivodeship. Alternatively, a derivative of the personal name Marc, Marcz, a pet form of Marcin, Marek (see Martin , Mark ).

variants.name: Marciniak
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Eugeniusz, Ewa, Agnieszka, Bartosz, Boleslaw, Ignatius, Jaroslaw, Jerzy, Krystyna, Ryszard, Slawomir, Stanislawa.
Polish: patronymic from the personal name Marcin (see Martin ).

variants.name: Marak
meanings: Czech:
(Marák): from a pet form of the personal names Martin or Marek .
(Mařák): metronymic from the female personal name Máří, Czech form of Maria . It may also denoted the husband of a woman named Máří.

variants.name: Machain
meanings: Basque: Castilianized form of Matxain, a topographic or habitational name referring to a house named with a diminutive of the personal name Martin .

variants.name: Maertens
meanings: Flemish and Dutch: patronymic from the personal name Maerten, a variant of Martin .
North German (Märtens): see Martens .

variants.name: Marcin
meanings: Slovak, Rusyn (from Slovakia), and Polish: from Marcin, a personal name equivalent to Martin . In Poland this is the most usual form as a personal name, but the surname is very rare there, while in Slovakia, where Marcin is a dialect form of the name Martin, the situation is opposite.
American shortened form of Polish Marcinkiewicz and other derivatives of the personal name Marcin (see 1 above).
In some cases possibly also French: from a vernacular form of the personal name Marcien, derived from the Latin surname Marcianus, popularized through the cult of a local saint.

variants.name: Macho
meanings: Spanish: nickname from macho ‘male; mule’ (from Latin masculus ‘male, masculine’), applied either to denote a virile, strong man or alternatively an idiot.
Spanish: possibly also a nickname for a forceful person or a metonymic occupational name for a smith, from macho ‘sledgehammer’ (from Latin marculus ‘hammer’).
Slovak: from a derivative of the personal name   Martin  .

variants.name: Marcinowski
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Aleksander, Bogdan, Jerzy, Zbigniew, Zigmund.
Polish: habitational name for someone from any of the places called Marcinów, Marcinowa, Marcinowice, or any of numerous other places in Poland named with the personal name Marcin (see Martin ).

variants.name: Marcinkiewicz
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Henryk, Beata, Elzbieta, Janina.
Polish: patronymic from the personal name Marcinek, a petform of Marcin (see Martin ).

variants.name: Mar
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Gim, Wai, Fong, Hong, Man Chung, Moo, Mun, Quon, Wing, Yat, Bok, Chun.
Spanish, Portuguese, Galician, Asturian-Leonese, and Catalan: topographic name for someone living by the sea, from mar ‘sea’ (from Latin mare).
German: nickname from Middle High German mar(w) ‘tender, delicate’, or an altered form of Mahr, a topographic name from mar ‘lake, swamp, stagnant water’.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Martin VI, Baron of Athelhampton was born in 1410, in Athelhampton, Dorset, England as the son of Lady Agnes de Loundres. He married Elizabeth Pydel in 1429, in Dorset, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1470, in Athelhampton, Dorset, England, at the age of 60, and was buried in Athelhampton, Dorset, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Martin VI, Baron of Athelhampton est né en 1410, à Athelhampton, Dorset, Angleterre, fils de Lady Agnes de Loundres. Il a épousé Elizabeth Pydel en 1429, à Dorset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1470, à Athelhampton, Dorset, Angleterre, à l’âge de 60 ans, et a été enterré à Athelhampton, Dorset, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Martin VI, Baron of Athelhamptonは1410年にイギリスドールセトアスエルハンプトンでLady Agnes de Loundresの息子として生まれた。1429年にイギリスイギリスドールセトでElizabeth Pydelと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1470年にイギリスドールセトアスエルハンプトンで60歳で死去し，イギリスドールセトアスエルハンプトンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Martin VI, Baron of Athelhampton(은)는 1410년 영국 도르세트 앳헬함프턴에서 Lady Agnes de Loundres의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1429년 영국 잉글랜드 도르세트에서 Elizabeth Pydel(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1470년 영국 도르세트 앳헬함프턴에서 60세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 도르세트 앳헬함프턴에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Martin VI, Baron of Athelhampton родился в 1410 г. в местности ателхамптон, дорсет, Англия. Его мать звали Lady Agnes de Loundres. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Pydel в 1429 г. в местности дорсет, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1470 г. в местности ателхамптон, дорсет, Англия в возрасте 60 лет и был похоронен в местности ателхамптон, дорсет, Англия.
